integrity.description: The agent used the OpenSIS web interface rather than terminal commands or direct database modifications.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser navigating the OpenSIS interface, filling out forms, and clicking buttons, rather than a terminal window executing SQL or curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually entered the data into the OpenSIS application and didn't just type the expected output into a text editor or mock HTML page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The surrounding UI elements (menus, headers, URL bar) match the OpenSIS application and show realistic system feedback (e.g., success messages after saving).

integrity.description: The agent did not simply manipulate the DOM to fake the final screenshot.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a logical progression of screenshots showing navigation through the OpenSIS menus (e.g., Staff -> Add Staff, Scheduling -> Courses, Grades -> Gradebook) rather than jumping straight to a faked success screen.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Staff Member: Jennifer Torres, Email: jtorres@riverside.edu, Role: Teacher. Course: Spanish III, Code: SPAN301, Subject: Foreign Language, Grade Level: 10, Credits: 1.0. Assignment: Placement Test. Grades: Carlos Mendez (82), Ana Nguyen (89). Pass threshold: 60.
